title: cachrys trifida var. aragonensis
intro: Cachrys trifida var. aragonensis, also known as Spanish Burnett, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Spain, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Spanish Burnett are said to include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. Herbal preparations of Cachrys trifida var. aragonensis typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although modern research on its efficacy and safety is limited.
